what_it_does: Declares one RELATION_TYPE dict registering the 'attached_to' board edge-kind: a directed item->channel/Space link with a label and description.
observations: desc states the item is attached to a channel/Space (item -> channel).
desc notes it mirrors the CHANNEL-LAYER-SEAM attachments manifest one level down.
desc emphasizes the binding is a typed edge, not a flat field.
standouts: Description frames the edge as the noticeboard analogue of the channel-layer attachments manifest.
conventions: Single module-level RELATION_TYPE constant with id matching the filename (attached_to.py).
Provides only the required keys (id, directed) plus optional label/desc; no inverse/near/far.
notes: One row file: the 'attached_to' typed edge binding a board item to a channel/Space.
questions: What is the CHANNEL-LAYER-SEAM attachments manifest it mirrors, and where is it defined?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — it registers exactly the attached_to edge-kind the board_edges constitution lists.
intention_signals: Tries to make 'item is attached to a channel' a first-class typed, validated relationship rather than an implicit field value.
contribution: Contributes the 'attached_to' edge-kind row that lets a board item declare a typed attachment to a channel/Space.
summary_for_embedding: A board_edges row declaring the directed 'attached_to' edge-kind linking a noticeboard item to a channel/Space.
intention_for_embedding: To register attaching a board item to a channel as a typed validated edge rather than a flat field.
